# SRC063 - The Zero-Price Effect in Freemium Business Models

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Pricing & Offers |
| Authors / source | Niemand et al. |
| Year | 2019 |
| Source type | Academic paper |
| Quality | Medium-High |
| URL | [https://www.researchgate.net/publication/331976105_The_zero-price_effect_in_freemium_business_models_The_moderating_effects_of_free_mentality_and_price-quality_inference](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/331976105_The_zero-price_effect_in_freemium_business_models_The_moderating_effects_of_free_mentality_and_price-quality_inference) |

## Bias / limitation

Academic; transfer by category.

## Core takeaway

Free can accelerate adoption but affect perceived value and conversion boundaries.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use when designing freemium/free-trial offers.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.
